title: Test statistic in a two-sample t test
description: Calculate the test statistic in a two sample t test for the difference of means.
topic_path: Testing for the difference of two population means
Test statistic in a two-sample t test

assessment_items.question_content:
Sophie was curious if her company's jumbo brown eggs and jumbo white eggs had different weights. She obtained two independent random samples of jumbo eggs, one of brown eggs and one of white eggs. Here is a summary of the results:

 | Brown | White
- | -: | -:
Mean | $71.2\text{ g}$ | $70.5\text{ g}$
Standard deviation | $1.2\text{ g}$ |$0.8\text{ g}$
Number of eggs | $490$ | $496$

Sophie wants to use these results to carry out a two-sample $t$ test to determine if the mean weights are significantly different for these types of eggs. Assume that all conditions have been met.

**Which of the following would be an appropriate test statistic for Sophie's test?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Formula for the test statistic

$\begin{align}
t&=\dfrac{\text{sample difference}-\text{hypothesized difference}}{\text{standard error of the difference}} \\\\
&=\dfrac{(\bar x_1 -\bar x_2)-(\mu_1-\mu_2)}{\sqrt{\dfrac{s_1^2}{n_1}+\dfrac{s_2^2}{n_2}}}
\end{align}$

The difference $\mu_1-\mu_2$ comes from the null hypothesis. In this type of test, we assume no difference in the population means, so we can substitute $0$ for $\mu_1-\mu_2$.

$\bar x_1$ and $\bar x_2$ are the two sample means.

$s_1$ and $s_2$ are the two sample standard deviations.

$n_1$ and $n_2$ are the two sample sizes.

###Components of formula

 | Brown | White
- | -: | -:
Mean | $\blueD{71.2}\text{ g}$ | $\greenD{70.5}\text{ g}$
Standard deviation | $\purpleD{1.2}\text{ g}$ |$\goldD{0.8}\text{ g}$
Number of eggs | $\maroonD{490}$ | $\tealD{496}$

$t=\dfrac{(\blueD{71.2}-\greenD{70.5})-0}{\sqrt{\dfrac{\purpleD{1.2}^2}{\maroonD{490}}+\dfrac{\goldD{0.8}^2}{\tealD{496}}}}$

###Answer

An appropriate test statistic would be:

$t=\dfrac{(71.2-70.5)-0}{\sqrt{\dfrac{1.2^2}{490}+\dfrac{0.8^2}{496}}}$

assessment_items.question_content:
For a study on teen social habits, Nolan is interested in seeing if there's a difference, between two schools, in the number of texts students sent this past Friday. He randomly samples students from each of these two schools. Here is a summary of the results:

 | School A | School B
- | -: | -:
Mean | $45$ texts | $36$ texts
Standard deviation | $6$ texts | $5$ texts
Number of students | $40$ | $40$

Nolan wants to use these results to carry out a two-sample $t$ test to determine if the mean numbers of texts are significantly different for these schools. Assume that all conditions have been met.

**Which of the following would be an appropriate test statistic for Nolan's test?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Formula for the test statistic

$\begin{align}
t&=\dfrac{\text{sample difference}-\text{hypothesized difference}}{\text{standard error of the difference}} \\\\
&=\dfrac{(\bar x_1 -\bar x_2)-(\mu_1-\mu_2)}{\sqrt{\dfrac{s_1^2}{n_1}+\dfrac{s_2^2}{n_2}}}
\end{align}$

The difference $\mu_1-\mu_2$ comes from the null hypothesis. In this type of test, we assume no difference in the population means, so we can substitute $0$ for $\mu_1-\mu_2$.

$\bar x_1$ and $\bar x_2$ are the two sample means.

$s_1$ and $s_2$ are the two sample standard deviations.

$n_1$ and $n_2$ are the two sample sizes.

###Components of formula

 | School A | School B
- | -: | -:
Mean | $\blueD{45}$ texts | $\greenD{36}$ texts
Standard deviation | $\purpleD{6}$ texts | $\goldD{5}$ texts
Number of students | $\maroonD{40}$ | $\tealD{40}$

$t=\dfrac{(\blueD{45}-\greenD{36})-0}{\sqrt{\dfrac{\purpleD{6}^2}{\maroonD{40}}+\dfrac{\goldD{5}^2}{\tealD{40}}}}$

###Answer

An appropriate test statistic would be:

$t=\dfrac{(45-36)-0}{\sqrt{\dfrac{6^2}{40}+\dfrac{5^2}{40}}}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Mio, a restaurant owner, wants to test if her two managers perform at the same level. To do that, she collects data about the number of customer complaints at two random samples of shifts (one for each manager). Here is a summary of the results:

 | Manager A | Manager B
- | -: | -:
Mean | $4$ complaints | $5$ complaints
Standard deviation | $0.3$ complaints| $0.5$ complaints
Number of shifts | $19$ | $21$

Mio wants to use these results to carry out a two-sample $t$ test to determine if the mean numbers of complaints are significantly different for the two managers. Assume that all conditions have been met.

**Which of the following would be an appropriate test statistic for Mio's test?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Formula for the test statistic

$\begin{align}
t&=\dfrac{\text{sample difference}-\text{hypothesized difference}}{\text{standard error of the difference}} \\\\
&=\dfrac{(\bar x_1 -\bar x_2)-(\mu_1-\mu_2)}{\sqrt{\dfrac{s_1^2}{n_1}+\dfrac{s_2^2}{n_2}}}
\end{align}$

The difference $\mu_1-\mu_2$ comes from the null hypothesis. In this type of test, we assume no difference in the population means, so we can substitute $0$ for $\mu_1-\mu_2$.

$\bar x_1$ and $\bar x_2$ are the two sample means.

$s_1$ and $s_2$ are the two sample standard deviations.

$n_1$ and $n_2$ are the two sample sizes.

###Components of formula

 | Manager A | Manager B
- | -: | -:
Mean | $\blueD 4$ complaints | $\greenD 5$ complaints
Standard deviation | $\purpleD{0.3}$ complaints| $\goldD{0.5}$ complaints
Number of shifts | $\maroonD{19}$ | $\tealD{21}$

$t=\dfrac{(\blueD{4}-\greenD{5})-0}{\sqrt{\dfrac{\purpleD{0.3}^2}{\maroonD{19}}+\dfrac{\goldD{0.5}^2}{\tealD{21}}}}$

###Answer

An appropriate test statistic would be:

$t=\dfrac{(4-5)-0}{\sqrt{\dfrac{0.3^2}{19}+\dfrac{0.5^2}{21}}}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Edvin studies a species of mosquitoes called Aedes albopictus. As a part of the study, he collects information about the length of each specimen in the group he is studying. Here is a summary of the results:

 | Male | Female
- | -: | -:
Mean | $5.1\text{ mm}$ | $6.3\text{ mm}$
Standard deviation | $1\text{ mm}$ | $1.2\text{ mm}$
Number of specimens | $206$ | $211$

Edvin wants to use these results to carry out a two-sample $t$ test to determine if the mean lengths are significantly different between males and females. Assume that all conditions have been met.

**Which of the following would be an appropriate test statistic for Edvin's test?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Formula for the test statistic

$\begin{align}
t&=\dfrac{\text{sample difference}-\text{hypothesized difference}}{\text{standard error of the difference}} \\\\
&=\dfrac{(\bar x_1 -\bar x_2)-(\mu_1-\mu_2)}{\sqrt{\dfrac{s_1^2}{n_1}+\dfrac{s_2^2}{n_2}}}
\end{align}$

The difference $\mu_1-\mu_2$ comes from the null hypothesis. In this type of test, we assume no difference in the population means, so we can substitute $0$ for $\mu_1-\mu_2$.

$\bar x_1$ and $\bar x_2$ are the two sample means.

$s_1$ and $s_2$ are the two sample standard deviations.

$n_1$ and $n_2$ are the two sample sizes.

###Components of formula

 | Male | Female
- | -: | -:
Mean | $\blueD{5.1}\text{ mm}$ | $\greenD{6.3}\text{ mm}$
Standard deviation | $\purpleD 1\text{ mm}$ | $\goldD{1.2}\text{ mm}$
Number of specimens | $\maroonD{206}$ | $\tealD{211}$

$t=\dfrac{(\blueD{5.1}-\greenD{6.3})-0}{\sqrt{\dfrac{\purpleD{1}^2}{\maroonD{206}}+\dfrac{\goldD{1.2}^2}{\tealD{211}}}}$

###Answer

An appropriate test statistic would be:

$t=\dfrac{(5.1-6.3)-0}{\sqrt{\dfrac{1^2}{206}+\dfrac{1.2^2}{211}}}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Amelie has balloons from two different brands. She wants to test the durability of each brand by measuring the volume of water that can be pumped into the balloons before they burst. Here is a summary of the results:

 | Brand A | Brand B
- | -: | -:
Mean | $10.2\text{ L}$ | $11.8\text{ L}$
Standard deviation | $1.2\text{ L}$ | $0.9\text{ L}$
Number of balloons | $8$ | $8$

Amelie wants to use these results to carry out a two-sample $t$ test to determine if the mean volumes are significantly different for the two brands. Assume that all conditions have been met.

**Which of the following would be an appropriate test statistic for Amelie's test?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Formula for the test statistic

$\begin{align}
t&=\dfrac{\text{sample difference}-\text{hypothesized difference}}{\text{standard error of the difference}} \\\\
&=\dfrac{(\bar x_1 -\bar x_2)-(\mu_1-\mu_2)}{\sqrt{\dfrac{s_1^2}{n_1}+\dfrac{s_2^2}{n_2}}}
\end{align}$

The difference $\mu_1-\mu_2$ comes from the null hypothesis. In this type of test, we assume no difference in the population means, so we can substitute $0$ for $\mu_1-\mu_2$.

$\bar x_1$ and $\bar x_2$ are the two sample means.

$s_1$ and $s_2$ are the two sample standard deviations.

$n_1$ and $n_2$ are the two sample sizes.

###Components of formula

 | Brand A | Brand B
- | -: | -:
Mean | $\blueD{10.2}\text{ L}$ | $\greenD{11.8}\text{ L}$
Standard deviation | $\purpleD{1.2}\text{ L}$ | $\goldD{0.9}\text{ L}$
Number of balloons | $\maroonD{8}$ | $\tealD{8}$

$t=\dfrac{(\blueD{10.2}-\greenD{11.8})-0}{\sqrt{\dfrac{\purpleD{1.2}^2}{\maroonD{8}}+\dfrac{\goldD{0.9}^2}{\tealD{8}}}}$

###Answer

An appropriate test statistic would be:

$t=\dfrac{(10.2-11.8)-0}{\sqrt{\dfrac{1.2^2}{8}+\dfrac{0.9^2}{8}}}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Jenny wants to compare the amount of weight people lose at her gym using two popular, non-overlapping weight-loss programs, Shred It and Burn It. She randomly samples people who use each program and tracks their weight loss. Here is a summary of the results:

 | Shred it | Burn it
- | -: | -:
Mean | $10\text{ kg}$ | $7\text{ kg}$
Standard deviation | $3.5\text{ kg}$ |$5.7\text{ kg}$
Number of people | $35$ | $35$

Jenny wants to use these results to carry out a two-sample $t$ test to determine if the mean weight losses are significantly different for the two programs. Assume that all conditions have been met.

**Which of the following would be an appropriate test statistic for Jenny's test?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Formula for the test statistic

$\begin{align}
t&=\dfrac{\text{sample difference}-\text{hypothesized difference}}{\text{standard error of the difference}} \\\\
&=\dfrac{(\bar x_1 -\bar x_2)-(\mu_1-\mu_2)}{\sqrt{\dfrac{s_1^2}{n_1}+\dfrac{s_2^2}{n_2}}}
\end{align}$

The difference $\mu_1-\mu_2$ comes from the null hypothesis. In this type of test, we assume no difference in the population means, so we can substitute $0$ for $\mu_1-\mu_2$.

$\bar x_1$ and $\bar x_2$ are the two sample means.

$s_1$ and $s_2$ are the two sample standard deviations.

$n_1$ and $n_2$ are the two sample sizes.

###Components of formula

 | Shred it | Burn it
- | -: | -:
Mean | $\blueD{10}\text{ kg}$ | $\greenD{7}\text{ kg}$
Standard deviation | $\purpleD{3.5}\text{ kg}$ |$\goldD{5.7}\text{ kg}$
Number of people | $\maroonD{35}$ | $\tealD{35}$

$t=\dfrac{(\blueD{10}-\greenD{7})-0}{\sqrt{\dfrac{\purpleD{3.5}^2}{\maroonD{35}}+\dfrac{\goldD{5.7}^2}{\tealD{35}}}}$

###Answer

An appropriate test statistic would be:

$t=\dfrac{(10-7)-0}{\sqrt{\dfrac{3.5^2}{35}+\dfrac{5.7^2}{35}}}$

assessment_items.question_content:
A market researcher suspected that employees at company A were older on average than employees at company B. They obtained a random sample of employee ages from each company. Here is a summary of the results:

 | Company A | Company B
- | -: | -:
Mean | $36.5$ | $32.0$
Standard deviation | $6.4$ |$5.6$
Number of employees | $30$ | $40$

The researcher  wants to use these results to test $\text{H}_0\text{: } \mu_\text{A}-\mu_\text{B}=0$ versus $\text{H}_{\text{a}}\text{: }\mu_\text{A}-\mu_\text{B}>0$. Assume that all conditions have been met.

**Which of the following would be an appropriate test statistic for their test?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Formula for the test statistic

$\begin{align}
t&=\dfrac{\text{sample difference}-\text{hypothesized difference}}{\text{standard error of the difference}} \\\\
&=\dfrac{(\bar x_1 -\bar x_2)-(\mu_1-\mu_2)}{\sqrt{\dfrac{s_1^2}{n_1}+\dfrac{s_2^2}{n_2}}}
\end{align}$

The difference $\mu_1-\mu_2$ comes from the null hypothesis. In this type of test, we assume no difference in the population means, so we can substitute $0$ for $\mu_1-\mu_2$.

$\bar x_1$ and $\bar x_2$ are the two sample means.

$s_1$ and $s_2$ are the two sample standard deviations.

$n_1$ and $n_2$ are the two sample sizes.

###Components of formula

They want to test $\text{H}_\text{a}\text{: }\mu_\text{A}-\mu_\text{B} > 0$, so we should use $\blueD{\bar x_{\text{A}}}-\greenD{\bar x_{\text{B}}}$ for the sample difference in the test statistic:

 | Company A | Company B
- | -: | -:
Mean | $\blueD{36.5}$ | $\greenD{32.0}$
Standard deviation | $\purpleD{6.4}$ |$\goldD{5.6}$
Number of employees | $\maroonD{30}$ | $\tealD{40}$

$t=\dfrac{(\blueD{36.5}-\greenD{32.0})-0}{\sqrt{\dfrac{\purpleD{6.4}^2}{\maroonD{30}}+\dfrac{\goldD{5.6}^2}{\tealD{40}}}}$

###Answer

An appropriate test statistic would be:

$t=\dfrac{(36.5-32.0)-0}{\sqrt{\dfrac{6.4^2}{30}+\dfrac{5.6^2}{40}}}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Shelby is skeptical of her friend's claim that Gerald's Café has much stronger coffee (in terms of caffeine content) than Sabine's Beans does. So Shelby takes a random sample of large coffees from both shops, and measures the amount of caffeine content in each coffee. Here is a summary of the results:

 | Sabine's Beans | Gerald's Café
- | -: | -:
Mean | $164\text{ mg}$ | $170\text{ mg}$
Standard deviation | $5.1\text{ mg}$ |$3.1\text{ mg}$
Number of cups| $37$ | $35$

Shelby wants to use these results to test $\text{H}_0\text{: } \mu_\text{Gerald}-\mu_\text{Sabine}=0$ versus $\text{H}_{\text{a}}\text{: }\mu_\text{Gerald}-\mu_\text{Sabine}>0$. Assume that all conditions have been met.

**Which of the following would be an appropriate test statistic for Shelby's test?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Formula for the test statistic

$\begin{align}
t&=\dfrac{\text{sample difference}-\text{hypothesized difference}}{\text{standard error of the difference}} \\\\
&=\dfrac{(\bar x_1 -\bar x_2)-(\mu_1-\mu_2)}{\sqrt{\dfrac{s_1^2}{n_1}+\dfrac{s_2^2}{n_2}}}
\end{align}$

The difference $\mu_1-\mu_2$ comes from the null hypothesis. In this type of test, we assume no difference in the population means, so we can substitute $0$ for $\mu_1-\mu_2$.

$\bar x_1$ and $\bar x_2$ are the two sample means.

$s_1$ and $s_2$ are the two sample standard deviations.

$n_1$ and $n_2$ are the two sample sizes.

###Components of formula

They want to test $\text{H}_\text{a}\text{: }\mu_\text{Gerald}-\mu_\text{Sabine} > 0$, so we should use $\blueD{\bar x_{\text{Gerald}}}-\greenD{\bar x_{\text{Sabine}}}$ for the sample difference in the test statistic:

 | Sabine's Beans | Gerald's Café
- | -: | -:
Mean | $\greenD{164}\text{ mg}$ | $\blueD{170}\text{ mg}$
Standard deviation | $\goldD{5.1}\text{ mg}$ |$\purpleD{3.1}\text{ mg}$
Number of cups| $\tealD{37}$ | $\maroonD{35}$

$t=\dfrac{(\blueD{170}-\greenD{164})-0}{\sqrt{\dfrac{\purpleD{3.1}^2}{\maroonD{35}}+\dfrac{\goldD{5.1}^2}{\tealD{37}}}}$

###Answer

An appropriate test statistic would be:

$t=\dfrac{(170-164)-0}{\sqrt{\dfrac{3.1^2}{35}+\dfrac{5.1^2}{37}}}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Arusi has a successful app which runs on the two common operating systems in the smartphone market. She wants to decide in which system to build new features by determining which system has users that spend more money.

So, she randomly samples users from each operating system and measures how much money they spent on the app in the past month. Here is a summary of the results:

 | System A | System B
- | -: | -:
Mean | $\$10$ | $\$15$
Standard deviation | $\$3$ |$\$5$
Number of users | $67$ | $65$

Arusi wants to use these results to test $\text{H}_0\text{: } \mu_\text{B}-\mu_\text{A}=0$ versus $\text{H}_{\text{a}}\text{: }\mu_\text{B}-\mu_\text{A}>0$. Assume that all conditions have been met.

**Which of the following would be an appropriate test statistic for Arusi's test?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Formula for the test statistic

$\begin{align}
t&=\dfrac{\text{sample difference}-\text{hypothesized difference}}{\text{standard error of the difference}} \\\\
&=\dfrac{(\bar x_1 -\bar x_2)-(\mu_1-\mu_2)}{\sqrt{\dfrac{s_1^2}{n_1}+\dfrac{s_2^2}{n_2}}}
\end{align}$

The difference $\mu_1-\mu_2$ comes from the null hypothesis. In this type of test, we assume no difference in the population means, so we can substitute $0$ for $\mu_1-\mu_2$.

$\bar x_1$ and $\bar x_2$ are the two sample means.

$s_1$ and $s_2$ are the two sample standard deviations.

$n_1$ and $n_2$ are the two sample sizes.

###Components of formula

They want to test $\text{H}_\text{a}\text{: }\mu_\text{B}-\mu_\text{A} > 0$, so we should use $\blueD{\bar x_{\text{B}}}-\greenD{\bar x_{\text{A}}}$ for the sample difference in the test statistic:

 | System A | System B
- | -: | -:
Mean | $\$\greenD{10}$ | $\$\blueD{15}$
Standard deviation | $\$\goldD{3}$ |$\$\purpleD{5}$
Number of users | $\tealD{67}$ | $\maroonD{65}$

$t=\dfrac{(\blueD{15}-\greenD{10})-0}{\sqrt{\dfrac{\purpleD{5}^2}{\maroonD{65}}+\dfrac{\goldD{3}^2}{\tealD{67}}}}$

###Answer

An appropriate test statistic would be:

$t=\dfrac{(15-10)-0}{\sqrt{\dfrac{5^2}{65}+\dfrac{3^2}{67}}}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Kaito grows tomatoes in two separate fields. When the tomatoes are ready to be picked, he is curious as to whether the sizes of his tomato plants differ between the two fields. He takes a random sample of plants from each field and measures the heights of the plants. Here is a summary of the results:

 | Field A | Field B
- | -: | -:
Mean | $1.3\text{ m}$ | $1.6\text{ m}$
Standard deviation | $0.5\text{ m}$ |$0.3\text{ m}$
Number of plants | $22$ | $24$

Kaito wants to use these results to test $\text{H}_0\text{: } \mu_\text{B}-\mu_\text{A}=0$ versus $\text{H}_{\text{a}}\text{: }\mu_\text{B}-\mu_\text{A}>0$. Assume that all conditions have been met.

**Which of the following would be an appropriate test statistic for Kaito's test?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Formula for the test statistic

$\begin{align}
t&=\dfrac{\text{sample difference}-\text{hypothesized difference}}{\text{standard error of the difference}} \\\\
&=\dfrac{(\bar x_1 -\bar x_2)-(\mu_1-\mu_2)}{\sqrt{\dfrac{s_1^2}{n_1}+\dfrac{s_2^2}{n_2}}}
\end{align}$

The difference $\mu_1-\mu_2$ comes from the null hypothesis. In this type of test, we assume no difference in the population means, so we can substitute $0$ for $\mu_1-\mu_2$.

$\bar x_1$ and $\bar x_2$ are the two sample means.

$s_1$ and $s_2$ are the two sample standard deviations.

$n_1$ and $n_2$ are the two sample sizes.

###Components of formula

They want to test $\text{H}_\text{a}\text{: }\mu_\text{B}-\mu_\text{A} > 0$, so we should use $\blueD{\bar x_{\text{B}}}-\greenD{\bar x_{\text{A}}}$ for the sample difference in the test statistic:

 | Field A | Field B
- | -: | -:
Mean | $\greenD{1.3}\text{ m}$ | $\blueD{1.6}\text{ m}$
Standard deviation | $\goldD{0.5}\text{ m}$ |$\purpleD{0.}3\text{ m}$
Number of plants | $\tealD{22}$ | $\maroonD{24}$

$t=\dfrac{(\blueD{1.6}-\greenD{1.3})-0}{\sqrt{\dfrac{\purpleD{0.3}^2}{\maroonD{24}}+\dfrac{\goldD{0.5}^2}{\tealD{22}}}}$

###Answer

An appropriate test statistic would be:

$t=\dfrac{(1.6-1.3)-0}{\sqrt{\dfrac{0.3^2}{24}+\dfrac{0.5^2}{22}}}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Researchers wanted to see if people who exercise regularly sleep better than people who don't. They took a random sample of adult males and surveyed them about their exercise routine and their sleep duration. Here is a summary of the results:

 | Exercise | No exercise
- | -: | -:
Mean | $7\text{ h}$ | $6.5\text{ h}$
Standard deviation | $1\text{ h}$ |$1.5\text{ h}$
Number of people | $180$ | $190$

The researchers want to use these results to test $\text{H}_0\text{: } \mu_\text{Exercise}-\mu_\text{No exercise}=0$ versus $\text{H}_{\text{a}}\text{: }\mu_\text{Exercise}-\mu_\text{No exercise}>0$. Assume that all conditions have been met.

**Which of the following would be an appropriate test statistic for the researchers' test?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Formula for the test statistic

$\begin{align}
t&=\dfrac{\text{sample difference}-\text{hypothesized difference}}{\text{standard error of the difference}} \\\\
&=\dfrac{(\bar x_1 -\bar x_2)-(\mu_1-\mu_2)}{\sqrt{\dfrac{s_1^2}{n_1}+\dfrac{s_2^2}{n_2}}}
\end{align}$

The difference $\mu_1-\mu_2$ comes from the null hypothesis. In this type of test, we assume no difference in the population means, so we can substitute $0$ for $\mu_1-\mu_2$.

$\bar x_1$ and $\bar x_2$ are the two sample means.

$s_1$ and $s_2$ are the two sample standard deviations.

$n_1$ and $n_2$ are the two sample sizes.

###Components of formula

They want to test $\text{H}_{\text{a}}\text{: }\mu_\text{Exercise}-\mu_\text{No exercise}>0$, so we should use $\blueD{\bar x_{\text{Exercise}}}-\greenD{\bar x_{\text{No exercise}}}$ for the sample difference in the test statistic:

 | Exercise | No exercise
- | -: | -:
Mean | $\blueD{7}\text{ h}$ | $\greenD{6.5}\text{ h}$
Standard deviation | $\purpleD{1}\text{ h}$ |$\goldD{1.5}\text{ h}$
Number of people | $\maroonD{180}$ | $\tealD{190}$

$t=\dfrac{(\blueD{7}-\greenD{6.5})-0}{\sqrt{\dfrac{\purpleD{1}^2}{\maroonD{180}}+\dfrac{\goldD{1.5}^2}{\tealD{190}}}}$

###Answer

An appropriate test statistic would be:

$t=\dfrac{(7-6.5)-0}{\sqrt{\dfrac{1^2}{180}+\dfrac{1.5^2}{190}}}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Researchers at a pharmaceutical company want to test a new pain-relief medicine. Specifically, they want to see if its effect takes less time than their old medicine.

They take a sample of people who suffer from chronic pain and randomly assign them into two groups. The first group receive the old medicine while the second group receive the new medicine (the participants don't know which medicine they have).

Then, the participants are asked to measure the time since they take the medicine until their pain is gone. Here is a summary of the results:

 | Old medicine | New medicine
- | -: | -:
Mean | $22\text{ min}$ | $19\text{ min}$
Standard deviation | $4\text{ min}$ |$2\text{ min}$
Number of people | $31$ | $29$

The researchers want to use these results to test $\text{H}_0\text{: } \mu_\text{Old}-\mu_\text{New}=0$ versus $\text{H}_{\text{a}}\text{: }\mu_\text{Old}-\mu_\text{New}>0$. Assume that all conditions have been met.

**Which of the following would be an appropriate test statistic for the researchers' test?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Formula for the test statistic

$\begin{align}
t&=\dfrac{\text{sample difference}-\text{hypothesized difference}}{\text{standard error of the difference}} \\\\
&=\dfrac{(\bar x_1 -\bar x_2)-(\mu_1-\mu_2)}{\sqrt{\dfrac{s_1^2}{n_1}+\dfrac{s_2^2}{n_2}}}
\end{align}$

The difference $\mu_1-\mu_2$ comes from the null hypothesis. In this type of test, we assume no difference in the population means, so we can substitute $0$ for $\mu_1-\mu_2$.

$\bar x_1$ and $\bar x_2$ are the two sample means.

$s_1$ and $s_2$ are the two sample standard deviations.

$n_1$ and $n_2$ are the two sample sizes.

###Components of formula

They want to test $\text{H}_{\text{a}}\text{: }\mu_\text{Old}-\mu_\text{New}>0$, so we should use $\blueD{\bar x_{\text{Old}}}-\greenD{\bar x_{\text{New}}}$ for the sample difference in the test statistic:

 | Old medicine | New medicine
- | -: | -:
Mean | $\blueD{22}\text{ min}$ | $\greenD{19}\text{ min}$
Standard deviation | $\purpleD{4}\text{ min}$ |$\goldD{2}\text{ min}$
Number of people | $\maroonD{31}$ | $\tealD{29}$

$t=\dfrac{(\blueD{22}-\greenD{19})-0}{\sqrt{\dfrac{\purpleD{4}^2}{\maroonD{31}}+\dfrac{\goldD{2}^2}{\tealD{29}}}}$

###Answer

An appropriate test statistic would be:

$t=\dfrac{(22-19)-0}{\sqrt{\dfrac{4^2}{31}+\dfrac{2^2}{29}}}$
